QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_43

question.text: SEBI mandated that market infrastructure institutions (MIIs) must use uniform transaction charges, eliminating volume-based slab discounts, effective from:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In July 2024, SEBI mandated MIIs to use uniform transaction charges, eliminating volume-based slab discounts for brokers. This was done to increase transparency for end investors. **The Trap:** Previously, high-volume brokers got slab discounts on exchange charges, which were not always passed on to clients. Uniform charges ensure all clients pay the same rate regardless of their broker's volume. **Cross-Exam Context:** This regulatory reform affects cost structure across all NISM exam domains. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** True-to-label charges = what the broker pays is what the client pays (for exchange charges). This reform ended the practice of brokers earning hidden margins on exchange transaction charges through volume discounts.
